title: Huang's Conjecture (4-coloring $P_6$-free graphs)
statement_text: The 4-coloring problem can be solved in polynomial time for the class of $P_6$-free graphs.
context_text: Huang conjectured that 4-coloring $P_6$-free graphs is polynomial-time tractable. This was the sole remaining open case in the complexity classification of 4-coloring $H$-free graphs for connected $H$: Huang had already proved NP-completeness of 4-coloring $P_7$-free graphs and of $k$-coloring $P_6$-free graphs for $k \geq 5$.
paper_contribution: This paper (the first in a two-paper series) proves Theorem 8, a polynomial-time algorithm for extending an excellent starred precoloring of a $P_6$-free graph with bounded seed; together with Theorem 7 proved in the companion paper, these imply Theorem 6—a polynomial-time algorithm for the 4-precoloring extension problem on $P_6$-free graphs—whose corollary is a polynomial-time 4-coloring algorithm, fully proving Huang's conjecture.
notes: Conjecture explicitly attributed to Huang [6]; bibliography not included in the supplied text so attributed_year cannot be confirmed.
paper_title: Four-coloring $P_6$-free graphs. I. Extending an excellent precoloring
